skill.name: azure-communication-callingserver-java
skill.description: Azure Communication Services CallingServer (legacy) Java SDK. Note - This SDK is deprecated. Use azure-communication-callautomation instead for new projects. Only use this skill when maintaining legacy integrations.
skill.summary: Migration guide for deprecated Azure CallingServer SDK to Call Automation SDK
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 17 potential issues. All findings are false positives: (1) 'external_commands' triggered by markdown code blocks using backticks (`) - no shell execution present; (2) 'weak cryptographic algorithm' triggered by generic words like 'execute' in documentation. This is a documentation-only skill providing SDK migration guidance with no executable code.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Shell Command Detection in Markdown
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but this is triggered by markdown code fence syntax (```java, ```xml) used for documentation. No actual shell commands or code execution exists in this skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All backticks in the file are used for markdown code blocks, not shell execution. The pattern 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' is a known false positive for markdown documentation skills.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3 and 96. These are false positives triggered by generic words in the documentation (description field, 'execute' in text). No cryptographic code exists in this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain only documentation text. No cryptographic algorithms or security-related code is present - this is purely an SDK migration guide.

content.user_title: Migrate from Azure CallingServer to Call Automation
content.value_statement: This skill helps developers migrate from the deprecated Azure Communication CallingServer Java SDK to the new Call Automation SDK, ensuring legacy integrations continue working while enabling new features.
actual_capabilities: Provides class name mapping between deprecated CallingServer and new CallAutomation SDKs
Shows Maven dependency changes for migration
Identifies deprecated features and their replacements
limitations: Does not provide automated code migration tools
Does not include the new Call Automation SDK functionality
Does not support older SDK versions beyond the documented migration path
This SDK is deprecated - new projects should use azure-communication-callautomation

use_cases.title: Legacy SDK Migration
use_cases.description: Developer working with existing azure-communication-callingserver integration needs to migrate to the new Call Automation SDK
use_cases.target_user: Java developers maintaining Azure Communication Services integrations

use_cases.title: Understanding Deprecation
use_cases.description: Technical lead evaluating migration effort for teams using the deprecated CallingServer SDK
use_cases.target_user: Technical leads and architects planning Azure SDK migrations

use_cases.title: Reference Guide
use_cases.description: Developer looking up class name mappings and API changes between old and new SDK versions
use_cases.target_user: Java developers working with Azure Communication Services

prompt_templates.title: Basic Migration Question
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I migrate from azure-communication-callingserver to azure-communication-callautomation in Java?
prompt_templates.scenario: When developer needs quick migration guidance

prompt_templates.title: Client Creation Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the changes needed for CallingServerClientBuilder to CallAutomationClientBuilder?

prompt_templates.title: Recording API Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I update my recording code from the deprecated StartRecordingOptions to the new Call Automation recording API?

prompt_templates.title: Full Migration Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: What are all the class name changes and API differences between the deprecated CallingServer SDK and the Call Automation SDK for Java?

output_examples.input: How do I migrate from azure-communication-callingserver to azure-communication-callautomation?
output_examples.output: Replace the dependency from azure-communication-callingserver (version 1.0.0-beta.5) to azure-communication-callautomation (version 1.6.0). Update your imports from com.azure.communication.callingserver to com.azure.communication.callautomation. Replace CallingServerClient with CallAutomationClient and CallingServerClientBuilder with CallAutomationClientBuilder.

output_examples.input: What replaced CallingServerClient in the new SDK?
output_examples.output: CallingServerClient was replaced by CallAutomationClient. The builder pattern remains similar but uses CallAutomationClientBuilder instead of CallingServerClientBuilder.

best_practices: Migrate to azure-communication-callautomation for new projects - the CallingServer SDK is deprecated
Review the trigger phrases ("callingserver legacy", "deprecated calling SDK") to activate this skill appropriately
Use this skill alongside the azure-communication-callautomation-java skill for complete migration guidance
anti_patterns: Do not use this SDK for new development - it is deprecated and will not receive updates
Do not mix CallingServer and CallAutomation classes in the same application
Do not assume ServerCall class exists in the new SDK - it was removed

faq.question: Is azure-communication-callingserver still supported?
faq.answer: No, this SDK is deprecated. Microsoft recommends using azure-communication-callautomation for all new projects.

faq.question: What is the main difference between the two SDKs?
faq.answer: The CallingServer SDK was renamed to Call Automation SDK with improved features and simplified class names.

faq.question: Can I use both SDKs in the same project?
faq.answer: It is not recommended to mix both SDKs. Migrate completely to Call Automation for consistency.

faq.question: Where do I find the new Call Automation SDK?
faq.answer: The new SDK is available as com.azure:azure-communication-callautomation on Maven Central.

faq.question: What happened to the ServerCall class?
faq.answer: ServerCall was removed. Use CallConnection for managing call operations in the new SDK.

faq.question: Does this skill help with new Call Automation features?
faq.answer: No, this skill only covers migration from the deprecated SDK. Use the azure-communication-callautomation-java skill for new features.
